response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, hands-on senior engineer with roughly 19 years of experience spanning web, embedded, and high-throughput systems, and recent exposure to AI-agent tooling (MCP code generators). However, the role is a director-level IC focused on AI strategy, and the resume shows no recognized AI/ML expertise, no shipped AI products or platforms of note, and no evidence of operating at principal/director scope — influencing org-wide technical strategy, advising strategic customers, or leading disruptive initiatives. His trajectory is consistently senior/staff-level product engineering rather than engineering leadership or AI innovation.
strengths: 19+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency distributed systems
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform with real-world adoption
Recent hands-on work on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, plus a history of teaching and public speaking
concerns: No demonstrated AI/ML depth or recognized AI expertise — AI exposure is recent and tooling-adjacent, not product- or research-level
No evidence of director/principal-level scope: strategy setting, cross-org influence, or advising executive customers
No advanced degree in an AI-related field; career has plateaued at senior/staff IC roles

key_factors.factor: Lack of recognized AI expertise and AI-built products
Seniority gap versus director-level strategy role
Long, broad engineering experience with startup/founder background
